page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores operating at a base frequency of 2.5 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 33 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX and AVX-512F instruction sets. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.se1.2xlarge exhibits modest processing capabilities. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests falls within the bottom 25%, indicating limited raw compute power. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, register in the bottom 10%. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks performs in the middle 50%, as do Passmark CPU extended instructions and physics tests. Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference also achieve average tier results, though larger model inference and database operations remain weak.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.se1.2xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand high-frequency CPU performance. The high memory density relative to the 8 vCPUs makes it suitable for caching layers, in-memory databases with moderate transaction volumes, and general-purpose workloads requiring a larger memory footprint without intensive computational overhead.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz on an x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Network bandwidth is limited to a baseline of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench scores in the bottom 25%. However, large-block memory bandwidth and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads with low compute demands, such as caching layers, moderate in-memory databases, and general-purpose applications requiring a larger memory footprint.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.se1.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, offering average memory bandwidth but weak overall compute performance.

tagline: An x86 virtual server offering high memory density and average memory bandwidth for workloads with moderate computational requirements.
